front: Philosopher: created the Wissenschaftslehre, built on the principle 'the I posits itself absolutely' and the concept of Tathandlung (fact/act)
Philosopher: argued consciousness cannot be grounded in the 'thing in itself' and should be founded on 'pure selfhood'; the I discovers limits via Anstoss (check)
Work: published on Kant's recommendation in 1792, initially mistaken for Kant's own work; argues God must be consistent with moral law
back: An Attempt at a Critique of All Revelation (Fichte)
work: An Attempt at a Critique of All Revelation

front: Philosopher: expelled from the University of Jena on charges of atheism after identifying God with the moral order in 'On the Ground of Our Belief'
Philosopher: Friedrich Jacobi coined 'nihilism' in an open letter attacking this thinker during the atheism dispute
Work: argues a person understands their identity only after being 'summoned' by another free individual; influenced Hegel's lord-bondsman dialectic
work: Foundations of Natural Right and Recognition

front: Philosopher: the thesis-antithesis-synthesis formula is often wrongly attributed to Hegel but actually originates with this thinker
Philosopher: Addresses to the German Nation promoted German unification against Napoleon; also wrote The Vocation of the Scholar and The Characteristics of the Present Age
